verdict_reasoning: TMED9 (transmembrane emp24 domain-containing protein 9) is a member of the p24/TMED family of type I transmembrane cargo receptors that reside in the early secretory pathway — predominantly ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and Golgi membranes. These proteins cycle between ER and Golgi as part of COPI/COPII vesicle machinery, functioning in cargo sorting and quality control. The cytoplasmic tail contains COPI-binding motifs for Golgi retention. No isoform is stably trafficked to the plasma membrane. Checking contextual buckets: cell_state_induced — no documented PM display under stress/activation; tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence; lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal resident; dual_localization — no PM pool reported in surface proteomics or flow cytometry; stable_surface_attachment — secreted form not described. No therapeutic programs targeting TMED9 on the cell surface are known. The protein body is definitively endomembrane-resident.
